title: maurocenia frangula
intro: Maurocenia frangula, also known as Frangula miqueliana, is a plant species native to New Caledonia, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its purgative properties, which are due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Maurocenia frangula include its potential to treat constipation, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat liver and gallbladder issues. In herbal preparations, Maurocenia frangula is often used in the form of tea, tincture, or dried root, although its use should be carefully monitored due to its potential side effects.
